rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA BUSE VS. SIEGFRIED J.W. RUPPERT
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 5, 2017, line 5. DEFENDANT SIEGFRIED RUPPERT's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication Of Issues DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and establish that it is entitled to a judgment as a matter of law: (1) No relevant and applicable authorities are cited for the proposition that CCP 726 and CCP 580b are applicable to the facts of this case. All authorities cited are factually distinguishable; (2) Moving party failed to cite any authority or provide any evidence that the term "financial contribution" as used in the Premarital Agreement includes loans. In fact moving party failed to propose any definition of this term and relies on an unsupported argument that "the Premarital Contract is pain on its face" (Points and Authorities 12:26) without any suggestion as to what the plain meaning exactly is (3) Moving party fails to establish that the issue of ownership and/or breach of the Promissory Note was and should have been adjudicated in the German or California Family Court Actions. There is no argument, evidence or authority as to what "all" financial issues were (or should have been) resolved in a German Court under German law (e.g. was there a decision regarding any specific items of property, including the Note; were any specific items of property at issue in light of the finding of general validity of the Premarital Agreement, etc.); = (501/REQ)
